xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: 4th Floor, Court House, Lokmanya Tilak Marg, Dhobi Talao, Mumbai - 400002, Maharashtra, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: 4th Floor, Court House, Lokmanya Tilak Marg, Dhobi Talao, Mumbai - 400002, Maharashtra, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfContactPerson: Shri Vipin Chandra Sati, Executive Director Shri Amitkumar Prabhakar Mundhe, Company Secretary and Compliance Officer
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesGeneralDisclosureExplanat: SECTION A: GENERAL DISCLOSURES Note - “ NA” denotes “ Not Applicable” - across this Report. Question No.16 - email of contact person - 1. vipin.sati@riil.in 2. amitkumar.mundhe@riil.in Question No.26 - Total number of KMPs mentioned as 2 - Does not include Director designated as KMP. Question No.27 - Note: 3 (Three) employees who separated, are included in the turnover rate as stated in the above data for financial year 2025-26.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Land transport and transport via pipelines
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Reliance Industrial Infrastructure Limited (“RIIL” / "the Company”) continues to provide infrastructure support services which includes transportation of petroleum products, natural gas and raw water through pipelines and other infrastructure support services mainly to Reliance Industries Limited.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: The Company maintains formal and informal channels for engaging with communities. Community members may submit any concerns or grievances to the Company’s registered office or email address. Additionally, in the event of an emergency, complaints may be made by calling the toll-free / control room / helpline numbers, which are prominently displayed along the path of the pipelines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: There are no Investors (other than shareholders).
There are no Investors (other than shareholders).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli:
The Company has a designated Email-ID: investor_relations@riil.in for shareholders to enable them to raise their grievances. Shareholder grievances are resolved by the Company through its Share Transfer Agent (KFin Technologies Limited).
The mechanism for handling grievances can be accessed at: https://www.riil.in/grievance-management.html

The Company has a well-defined vigilance framework that provides a platform for employees and Directors to lodge their grievances/ complaints. The Company’s Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower Policy is available at https://www.riil.in/pdf/whistle-blower-policy.pdf
The Company has no workers.

The Company provide infrastructure support services which includes transportation of petroleum products, natural gas and raw water through pipelines and other infrastructure support services mainly to Reliance Industries Limited. Customers may raise their grievances via call, e-mail or letter.
Value chain partners may raise their grievances via call, e-mail or letter.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Any unforeseen leakages from pipelines or other incidents that may have negative impact on environment may adversely affect the business and local communities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
The Company uses a Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) system and a Leak Detection System to continuously monitor the petroleum pipelines. Continuous on- ground patrolling is carried out to protect the pipeline network, with real-time GPS-based tracking at the respective control room. The Emergency Response and Disaster Management Plan (ERDMP) prescribes Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to enable swift responses to any emergency or incident.
The Company ensures pipeline integrity through a comprehensive set of corrective and preventive measures. The cathodic protection system is maintained and continuously monitored to safeguard against corrosion, while specialized surveys & studies such as Inline Inspection (ILI), Magnetic Tomography Method (MTM), Direct Current Voltage Gradient (DCVG), Smart ball testing, surge analysis etc., are conducted to assess pipeline health, as per requirement. Preventive maintenance routines are applied to equipment like breathers and pumps (both diesel and electric-driven) for ensuring reliable operation. The line charging process is executed effectively and coating damages on the pipeline surface, if any, are promptly repaired. Whenever it is required to approve pipeline crossings, line locators are utilized to trace the pipeline and mark Right of Use boundaries on the ground and dig verification may also be performed for enabling issuance of NOCs to stakeholders. Together, these measures create a robust framework for maintaining the safety, reliability and efficiency of the pipeline system.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: With ever-growing global demand for energy and associated increase in costs, it would lead to operational risks due to non-availability of energy or increased energy costs if energy is not efficiently used.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company employs internal mechanisms for continuous tracking of key energy usage parameters aimed at improving energy efficiency and conservation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: It is crucial for the Company to improve its innovation capabilities and make use of cutting-edge technologies in order to maintain relevance in the constantly evolving macro- economic environment and achieve consistent development.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company strives to identify and deploy best-in-class, energy-efficient technological equipment while evaluating innovative solutions available in the industry for optimal product selection.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Catastrophes such as unpredictable weather patterns, cyclones, droughts and downpours are on the rise due to climate change and are proving to be a challenge globally. These unforeseen adverse conditions can be a risk to the organization’s operations and can lead to infrastructure damage and human loss.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
The Emergency Response and Disaster Management Plan (ERDMP) establishes Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for prompt incident response and is supported by a robust Integrated Disaster Recovery and Emergency Response system. Regular mock drills are undertaken to ensure operational readiness. Moreover, pipeline route information has been shared with the concerned public, local fire and police authorities.
The Company maintains a comprehensive contingency plan that includes risk assessments to identify vulnerable sections of the Pipelines and coordination with local authorities for rapid mobilization during crisis. Critical systems such as cathodic protection, pumps and monitoring equipment are safeguarded through preventive maintenance to minimize failure risks.
In the event of natural disasters, such as floods or earthquakes, rapid inspection and repair protocols are activated to ensure functionality and prevent service disruption.
Additionally, communication channels are established to keep stakeholders informed, while backup systems and alternative supply routes are prepared to ensure continuity of Pipeline operations.
Thus, the integrated disaster management framework ensures that the Pipelines remain secure, reliable and capable of withstanding unforeseen challenges.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: In order to provide its human capital with a working environment that places utmost emphasis on their mental and physical well-being, employee health and safety is a non-negotiable element for the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company maintains a comprehensive framework for managing operational and safety risks, supported by robust systems for periodic physical and virtual reviews and audits of its facilities. Participation in standards committees of statutory authorities, including Oil Industry Safety Directorate (OISD) and Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (PNGRB), reinforces the Company’s commitment to safe and compliant business practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Proper handling of sensitive data including personal information and business confidential data is of paramount importance to safeguard businesses, clients, infrastructure, and internal users from security threats. Any leakages or discrepancies could lead to adverse financial, reputational and operational impacts.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Cyber Security strategy at the Company is ‘Aligned to Business Objectives’ and ‘Calibrated to the Threat Landscape’. The Company adopts a defence-in-depth approach by implementing layered security technologies and processes to strengthen resilience against emerging and evolving cyber threats. Privacy by Design principles are incorporated across the personal data lifecycle, business processes and applications. The Company also undertakes regular internal and external attestations to benchmark its cybersecurity and privacy practices against applicable laws, regulations, industry best practices and standards, thereby ensuring compliance and enhanced assurance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Business Ethics, Integrity and Transparency, Code of Conduct and Grievance Handling Mechanisms
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ethics and integrity have always been the core values to acquire stakeholder trust and establish a strong reputation. These elements are essential for a Company in order to sustain over the long term. Any mishaps in business ethics or integrity can prove risky and can cause business instability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The decisions relating to the employee’s conduct are monitored through the Ethics and Compliance Task Force (ECTF), which comprises an Executive Director, the Company Secretary and the Chief Financial Officer of the Company. The Company has established a Vigil Mechanism and a Whistle-Blower policy for its employees and directors. The whistle-blower can make a protected disclosure through an e-mail or dedicated telephone line or a letter to the ECTF or to the Chairperson of the Audit Committee.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The foundation upon which the Company’s image is built is compliance. It is crucial for the Company to maintain regulatory compliance in order to foster confidence among its stakeholder groups and to ensure that its operations are compliant with applicable laws in order to prevent legal violations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company has adopted a digitally enabled comprehensive compliance management framework. Effective control and efficient oversight by the senior management is ensured by cascading the responsibility matrix till the last performer of the activity. The Company’s codes, training as well as focus on ensuring 100% compliance and continuous monitoring have enabled a mature, digitally-enabled compliance framework.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePoliciesExplanator: 1. Code of Business Conduct and ethics for Directors and Management Personnel https://www.riil.in/pdf/CodeBusiness-Conduct-ethics-amended-upto-jan2024.pdf 2. xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: The Company has framed policies that conform to different applicable statutes / guidelines / rules / policies etc., issued by Government of India from time to time. Industry practices and national / international standards such as BIS, PNGRB, PESO, OISD etc., as applicable, are widely adopted by the Company.

The Company has framed policies that conform to different applicable statutes / guidelines / rules / policies etc., issued by Government of India from time to time. Industry practices and national / international standards such as BIS, PNGRB, PESO, OISD etc., as applicable, are widely adopted by the Company.

Industry practices and national / international standards such as BIS, PNGRB, PESO, OISD etc., as applicable, are widely adopted by the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar:
The Company is committed to build trust through choices and decisions by doing the right thing, acting diligently at all times and encouraging employees to report actual or suspected violations of applicable laws and regulations and the Code of Conduct and Ethics - Values and Behaviours.
Respect for human rights is the cornerstone of the Company’s philosophy in which everyone feels included and gets equal opportunity to make a contribution.
The Company has set various commitments and goals. It has aligned its efforts to targets, community support and environment sustainability. It remains steadfast in its commitment to contribute towards achieving the goals.

The Company is committed to build trust through choices and decisions by doing the right thing, acting diligently at all times and encouraging employees to report actual or suspected violations of applicable laws and regulations and the Code of Conduct and Ethics - Values and Behaviours.
Respect for human rights is the cornerstone of the Company’s philosophy in which everyone feels included and gets equal opportunity to make a contribution.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible:
The Company places Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) at the core of its business operations. It accords the highest priority to occupational health, safety and environmental protection in and around its areas of operation while adhering to Corporate Governance principles to ensure transparency, integrity and accountability.
The Company considers the integration of Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) principles into its business practices a core responsibility. Through this integration, it enhances resilience, drives cultural transformation, systematically manages risks and safeguards the interests of all stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe:
Name: Shri Vipin Chandra Sati DIN: 10968198 Designation: Executive Director
Yes. The Company has constituted various committees, which are responsible to oversee key sustainability related policies, performance and issues of the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT:
Name: Shri Vipin Chandra Sati DIN: 10968198 Designation: Executive Director
Yes. The Company has constituted various committees, which are responsible to oversee key sustainability related policies, performance and issues of the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheAgencyIfTheEntityCarr: The policies pertaining to health, safety and environment have been audited by external agency viz., International Certification Services, an agency approved by Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board. As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

The policies pertaining to health, safety and environment have been audited by external agency viz., International Certification Services, an agency approved by Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board. As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

The policies pertaining to health, safety and environment have been audited by external agency viz., International Certification Services, an agency approved by Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board. As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.

As a good governance practice, policies are periodically reviewed and updated by concerned business heads in consultation with stakeholders and approved by the management and/or Board itself, as and when required.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesManagementAndProcessDiscl: SECTION B: MANAGEMENT AND PROCESS DISCLOSURES Question No.10 - for Performance against above policies and follow up action - Indicate whether review was undertaken by Director / Committee of the Board / Any other Committee - Yes, the Board / Committees of Board meet periodically to review the performance against the above policies. Question No.10 - for Compliance with statutory requirements of relevance to the principles and rectification of any non-compliances - Indicate whether review was undertaken by Director / Committee of the Board / Any other Committee - Yes, by the Board / Committees of Board. The Company is in compliance with applicable statutory requirements of relevance to the NGRBC Principles.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: The Company conducts familiarisation programmes for its Board of Directors at regular intervals which covers topics such as Corporate Governance Practices, update on Business operations, Emergency Response and Disaster Management Plan, ESG, cybersecurity and various other regulatory updates.

1.Code of Conduct and Ethics - Values and Behaviours – This training outlines the six values that form the foundation of the Code and helps employees understand the elements of vigil mechanism.
2.Anti-Bribery Management System (“ABMS”) describes the concept of bribery, how it happens and how employees can recognise the red flags of bribery. Thus, it helps employees prevent and overcome bribery and also uphold the Code of Conduct. It also guides employees on ABMS reporting.
3.Cybersecurity Awareness – This helps employees identify situations that may lead to breach of Cyber Security at Reliance and how they can demonstrate correct behaviour to protect the organization from external threats.
4.Creating a Respectful Workplace helps employees understand what constitutes sexual harassment at the workplace and the impact on the individual as well as the organisation thus preventing such instances. It guides employees on the mechanism for reporting sexual harassment.
5.Reliance Management System (“RMS”) – RMS is a set of Management Systems including Operations Management systems and Health, Safety, Environment and Fire policies. RMS training helps employees understand the concept and reasons for implementing it and understand how an employee can use RMS as a tool for self-development.

1.Code of Conduct and Ethics - Values and Behaviours – This training outlines the six values that form the foundation of the Code and helps employees understand the elements of vigil mechanism.
2.Anti-Bribery Management System (“ABMS”) describes the concept of bribery, how it happens and how employees can recognise the red flags of bribery. Thus, it helps employees prevent and overcome bribery and also uphold the Code of Conduct. It also guides employees on ABMS reporting.
3.Cybersecurity Awareness – This helps employees identify situations that may lead to breach of Cyber Security at Reliance and how they can demonstrate correct behaviour to protect the organization from external threats.
4.Creating a Respectful Workplace helps employees understand what constitutes sexual harassment at the workplace and the impact on the individual as well as the organisation thus preventing such instances. It guides employees on the mechanism for reporting sexual harassment.
5.Reliance Management System (“RMS”) – RMS is a set of Management Systems including Operations Management systems and Health, Safety, Environment and Fire policies. RMS training helps employees understand the concept and reasons for implementing it and understand how an employee can use RMS as a tool for self-development.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Being a responsible organisation, the Company has proactively taken measures in combating bribery and corruption. The Company has in place a mandatory e-learning course which aims to equip its employees with the required understanding and knowledge to effectively prevent, identify and respond to bribery risks. The Company believes that it can contribute to create a stronger and bribery-resistant business ecosystem. Towards this end, an e-learning module, has also been developed for the Company’s vendors. This will help vendors understand Anti-Bribery Management System better and also create the right levels of awareness about the Company’s expectations of ethical conduct of business.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText: SECTION C: PRINCIPLE WISE PERFORMANCE DISCLOSURE PRINCIPLE 1 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.1 - For workers - For Essential indicator question 11 of Principle 3, Workers include third party contractors. This definition is applicable to the said table only. Question No.1 - Note: All employees are strongly encouraged to complete 5 training courses annually. Question No.2 - Note: Does not include penalties by regulators in the ordinary course of business. Question No.8 - Note: Accounts payable include all trade payables excluding amount payable for cases where demand notes or invoices are not finalised due to ongoing negotiations. Question No.9 - For Concentration of purchases - The data for FY 2025-26 covers 100% of purchases (by value) as against 97% of purchases (by value) considered for FY 2024-25, accordingly, the figures are not directly comparable. For Concentration of Sales - The Company's revenue comes solely from infrastructure support services, which are mainly provided to Reliance Industries Limited. It does not sell through dealers or distributors. For Share of Related Party Transactions - a. Purchases (Purchases with related parties / Total Purchases) - Purchases with related parties excludes Reimbursements. Note: All the financial figures provided in Question No.9 are in Rupees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Currently, there are no products manufactured that will generate plastic waste at the end of life.
Currently, there are no products manufactured that will generate E-waste at the end of life.
Currently, there are no products manufactured that will generate hazardous waste at the end of life.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 2 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.2. a. - The Company has a well-established vendor selection process that addresses social, ethical and environmental considerations as mandated by law. The Company employs a transparent tendering process for vendor selection. The Company is also committed to support local communities and provides equal opportunities to MSMEs. Question No.2. b. - 91% and 79% of the total procurement of the Company's inputs were sourced from MSME and local (within 100 km of our operating locations) vendors, respectively.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: All employees and those eligible are provided with equal opportunities. The Company is committed to an inclusive work culture, without any discrimination. The policy in this regard is accessible to the internal stakeholders of the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: All employee grievances are addressed appropriately through multiple channels. The Company has a Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower policy under which the stakeholders are encouraged to report violations of applicable laws and regulations and the Code of Conduct and Ethics - Values and Behaviours – in confidence and without fear of any retaliation.

All employee grievances are addressed appropriately through multiple channels. The Company has a Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower policy under which the stakeholders are encouraged to report violations of applicable laws and regulations and the Code of Conduct and Ethics - Values and Behaviours – in confidence and without fear of any retaliation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: The Company has implemented an Operating Management System (“OMS”) which addresses aspects of Occupational Health and Safety including process safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The key element of OMS is identifying the hazards and assessing them supported by a Risk Matrix. These risks are reviewed on a periodical basis by cross functional operating teams. Task related hazards are identified and assessed using a Hazard Identification and Task Risk Assessment Process.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProcessesForWorkersToReportThe: Not applicable, since the Company does not have any workers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: Safety and health of employees is, has been and will continue to be paramount. Some specific actions taken include: a)Mandatory on job safety training is imparted to employees as per the criticality of the job and any change in procedural guidelines. b)Employees are proactively engaged and involved in aspects related to their safety and wellbeing. The employees are encouraged to report workplace related near-misses, unsafe acts and unsafe conditions. Identified gaps, if any, are rectified with necessary time bound actions. c)The operating managers continuously interact with the employees in the field to observe their behaviours, have a conversation to recognise their good behaviour or coach them to correct their behaviours if required and also listen to their feedback if they have any safety concerns. d)Safety and health are monitored on a continuous basis through rigorous inspections, data analysis and employee feedback, ensuring proactive risk mitigation. e)Annual / Periodic Medical Examination is done for all employees. f)Health awareness sessions, webinars and bulletins are circulated as part of ongoing commitment for health and wellbeing of employees. g)Risk registers and mitigation plans are made for keeping all jobs on check for safety of employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Incidents are reported through the Operating Management System (OMS), which are thereby investigated and tracked to closure.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 3 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.3 - Yes, there is every endeavour to ensure all requirements of any differently abled person working at any of the offices is completely provided for and met. Question No.5 - Note: None of the employees availed parental leave during the reporting period. Question No.6 - mechanism available to receive and redress grievances - for Permanent Workers and Other than Permanent Workers - NA Question No.10. d. - Yes, all employees are fully taken care of on account of all medical - exigencies or otherwise. Question No.11 - Note: For Essential indicator question 11, Workers include third party contractors. This definition is applicable to this table only. Question No.14 - Note: Statutory assessments related to aspects of health and safety practices and working conditions is periodically undertaken by the entity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Key Stakeholder groups are identified based on their materiality to the Company’s business operations along with the impact of their association with the company and the community at large. Our stakeholders are our employees, shareholders, customers, suppliers, NGOs and Communities and Government & Regulatory Authorities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: E-mails, SMS, Meetings, Surveys, Feedbacks, Letters, Website and Internal Portals
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc:
To understand employee needs and opinions.
To keep employees informed about the organisation’s plans and procedures.
Employee Safety and Wellbeing.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Stock exchange intimations, Newspapers, Media Releases, Website, Notice Board, Annual Report, Annual General Meeting, Letters, E-mails, SMS, Webinars, Chairman’s speech, NSE Electronic Application Processing System (NEAPS), BSE Listing Centre, SEBI Complaints Redress System (SCORES), Surveys, Depositories, Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) Portal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc:
To keep shareholders updated about the organisation’s performance and other corporate developments.
To bring transparency with the existing and potential shareholders.
To ensure efficient conduct of day to day business activities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: E-mails, Phone Calls, Meetings and through Annual Reports or Compliance Filings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: To ensure efficient conduct of day to day business activities.
Sharing of programme activities, Service Delivery Implementation, Community Social Work.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Website, portals, E-mails, phone calls, written communications, filings, representations, Meetings and through Annual Reports or Compliance Filings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherFrequencyOfEngag: Periodic as well as event-based compliances - quarterly, half-yearly, annually etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Engagement, advocacy, communication, collaboration for compliance with applicable regulatory framework and ease of doing business.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: The Company has a Vigil Mechanism and Whistle-Blower policy under which the stakeholders are encouraged to report violations of applicable laws and regulations and the Code of Conduct - in confidence and without fear of any retaliation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: The Company has a Vigil mechanism and Whistle-Blower policy and Prevention of Sexual Harassment policy to prevent any adverse consequences to the complainant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No corrective measures were required during the reporting period.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 5 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.3a - for Board of Directors - Does not include sitting fees paid to Non Executive Directors. Note: The figures mentioned in the given table are in Rupees. Question No.3a - for Key Managerial Personnel - Does not include director designated as KMP. Question No.3a - for Employees other than BoD and KMP - includes employees who separated during the year Question No 10 - Note: The offices are assessed by statutory authorities, appropriate authorities or internal auditors as deemed appropriate.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: The Company’s business is closed-loop transportation of petroleum products, natural gas and raw water through pipelines, hence there is no generation of waste in this activity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 6 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.1 - Note: The source for Purchasing Power Parity (PPP) is International Monetary Fund (IMF). The PPP rate considered for FY 2025-26 is 20.34 as per 2026 IMF update and for FY 2024-25 is 20.66 as per 2025 IMF update. Note: The product moves in the closed loop system of the pipeline from source to consumer, hence there is no energy intensity in terms of physical output. Question No.3 - Note: The water withdrawal mentioned in the above question is for human consumption and other non-operational purposes only. Question No.4 - Note: No water is used in the operations since the product moves in the closed loop system of the pipeline from source to consumer. Hence, there is zero discharge. Question No. 5 - Note: No water is used in the operations since the product moves in the closed loop system of the pipeline from source to consumer. Hence there is zero discharge. Question No.6 - Note: The Company’s business is closed-loop transportation of petroleum products, natural gas and raw water through pipelines, hence there are no emissions in this activity. Question No.7 - for FY 2024-25 - Figures for FY 2024-25 have been restated for improved accuracy and clarity. Question No.9 - Note: The Company’s business is closed-loop transportation of petroleum products, natural gas and raw water through pipelines, hence there is no generation of waste in this activity. Question No.12 - No Projects are executed in the current financial year
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple7ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 7 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.2 - No adverse order has been received by the Company from regulatory authorities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: The major needs of the community are in the form of access to quality and affordable healthcare services. Communication is through community meetings and in case of an emergency, complaints may be made by community members by calling the toll-free / control room / helpline numbers that are prominently displayed along the route of the pipelines. Community members can also send any concerns or grievances to the Company’s registered office or email address, which are addressed through a portal mechanism developed by the Company for resolving grievances in a just, fair and timely manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 8 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.1 - The Company has not undertaken any projects during the current financial year requiring SIA. Question No.2 - The Company has not undertaken any projects during the current financial year requiring R&R. Question No.4 - Note: The boundary of sourcing for MSMEs / small producers is Indian suppliers and for the input materials sourced directly from within India is global suppliers. Question No.5 - for FY 2024 25 - The above data is consistent with XBRL filing
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: Consumer complaints in general are handled through the local managers of the Company. In case of any complaint, the consumer intimates the Company which is thereafter resolved.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple9ExplanatoryText: PRINCIPLE 9 - ESSENTIAL INDICATORS Question No.5 - Yes. A comprehensive cybersecurity framework aligned with leading industry standards and best practices is leveraged. The data privacy policy is hosted at: https://www.riil.in/privacypolicy.html
